meaning: n.从前流通于欧洲各国的钱币达克特,硬币
examples: The stake is 1 Ducat and you can double it by winning.
Ducat tried to reach out to other mothers of diabetic kids, but at first no one wanted to talk.
In 1965, Lee Ducat was a Philadelphia homemaker with a child who had just been found to have diabetes.
Dundee Sheriff Court heard that Ducat could not even account for where the money had gone.
Ducat, 27, of Aberdeen, pleaded guilty to embezzling the money between 23 January and 5 November.
